title: Fuel Mixture Calibration
description: Dial in a safe oxidizer-to-fuel ratio for a hobbyist model rocket before launch.

dialogue.text: Phoenix here. We'll dial in a hobbyist propellant mix with precision and minimal waste.

options.text: Absolutely, let's prep the calibration bench.

dialogue.text: Before weighing anything: clear ignition sources, run ventilation, and keep emergency rinse nearby. No shortcuts around energetic materials.

options.text: Bench is clear, PPE is on, and ventilation is running.
I spot loose tools and clutter. We should reset the bench first.

dialogue.text: Good catch. Sweep the bench, cap all containers, and restart only after confirming the scale and containment tray are stable.

options.text: Bench reset complete. Resume controlled measurements.

dialogue.text: We'll weigh fuel and oxidizer. Grab a scale and tare the canister before dosing. Record the first ratio target as 68:32.

options.text: Scale ready, mixture measured to the first ratio target.

dialogue.text: Cross-check the ratio against your tolerance band (+/- 1%). If the blend drifts, branch into correction before we seal the batch.

options.text: Ratio verified in tolerance and test hardware is staged.
Ratio is out of band. Enter correction loop before launch prep.

dialogue.text: Add binder in tiny increments, remix, then re-weigh. If drift persists twice, discard the batch and restart from safe tare.
Nice work. This hobby-class mix should burn steady and clean for launch when paired with your verified hardware stack.

options.text: Calibration logged and ready for flight prep.
